results.title: 7 Best Loom Alternatives: Free & Paid
results.content:
The 7 best Loom alternatives in 2026 are Sybill (best for sales teams that need meeting recordings plus AI summaries, follow-ups, and CRM autofill), Vidyard (best for video prospecting and personalized sales videos), Tella (best for polished product demos and tutorials), mmhmm (best for engaging presentations with virtual backgrounds), Screenpal (best free screen recorder with basic editing), Vimeo Record (best for enterprise video hosting and analytics), and Komodo Decks (best for interactive video presentations). The key differentiator is whether you need simple screen recording or an end-to-end sales workflow — tools like Sybill replace Loom for revenue teams by not just recording but also generating AI summaries, auto-drafting follow-up emails, and syncing meeting intelligence to CRM. [...] Best for: Educators, YouTubers, trainers.

### 3. Screen Studio — Best for Mac-Only Cinematic Recordings

Screen Studio creates smooth, cinematic, Apple-like demo videos with almost no effort. It’s perfect for teams that care deeply about polish and run entirely on Mac.

Screen Studio as a Loom alternative

Source: Screen Studio website

Key features:

 Automatic zoom + motion
 Whisper AI captions
 Smooth cursor animations
 4K export support

Pros: Beautiful, professional output with intuitive presets.  
Cons: Mac-only; no cloud hosting.

Pricing: $29/mo or $9/mo billed annually.

Best for: Founders, product teams, demo creators.

### 4. Tella — Best for Creators & Educators Who Want Polished Layouts [...] The best Loom alternatives depend on what you’re trying to create. Choose Supademo for interactive demos and training, Camtasia for deep editing and professional tutorials, Tella for creator-friendly layouts, VEED for AI-powered social videos, and ScreenRec if you want a free Loom alternative with unlimited recording. If your workflow centers on sharing call snippets, summaries, and insights rather than recording your screen, Sybill is the strongest async alternative, turning meetings into instantly shareable knowledge. Each tool fills a gap Loom leaves open, making them powerful alternatives to Loom in 2026.

### Is Loom or Vimeo better?

# The Best LoomAlternatives

The best Loomalternatives areCap, Zight, FocuSee, Tella, and Bubbles.

Cap screenshot

Cap

Cap

3.9 ·

Choose Capif... [...] See details ↓

## What to Consider

Loom set the standard for async screen recording with a fast “record, share a link, get feedback” workflow, but the alternatives have splintered into distinct camps. Cap leans into open-source control and a modern viewing experience (custom domains, chapters, transcripts), Zight expands the idea into a broader visual-messaging toolkit with screenshots and GIFs, and Tella focuses on making demos feel more polished with multi-clip recording and presentation-friendly touches. FocuSee pushes toward “record → automatically edited” output for creators who want captions, zooms, and social-ready exports, while Bubbles reframes the space around meetings and collaborative review with AI notes and threaded, screen-specific comments. [...] ### Standout features

 ✓Multi-clip recording in one session
 ✓Built-in speaker notes
 ✓Selective window or area capture
 ✓Zoom controls for highlighting details
 ✓Presentation-focused video layouts

## Bubbles

BubblesBubbles

Teamwork. On your time.

4.9 · 38 reviews

Learn more →

Bubbles is built for teams that want async collaboration around recordings, not just a link in a chat thread. It combines screen recording with a workspace-like layer for threaded discussion, making it a distinct alternative to Loom for feedback-heavy workflows.

Where it really separates is annotation and commenting that can be anchored to specific areas of the screen and moments in the video. That’s especially useful for design reviews, QA, and UAT, where precise context prevents long clarification loops.

results.title: How To Choose The Best Loom Alternative In 2026 [Top 20 Reviewed] | Tella Blog
results.content:
Blog/

# How To Choose The Best Loom Alternative In 2026 [Top 20 Reviewed]

Compare the best Loom alternatives for recording, editing, sharing, and switching from Loom. See when Tella, Supercut, Cap, VEED, and other tools make sense.

Since writing our original list of Loom alternatives in 2022, the options for screen recording, editing, and caption creating have increased massively.

Thanks to both the advancement of each product and the acceleration of artificial intelligence (AI), we now have a market of advanced video recording providers that make it easy to create high-quality videos from the comfort of your home office. [...] ## Honorable mentions when looking for Loom alternatives

1. VokoscreenNG: A screencast creator that supports 39 languages to record the screen, an area, or a window. The software currently only supports Linux users.
2. Screen Studio: A native Mac app that lets you record your screen or screen and camera at the same time. Its key feature is animated zoom effects that are added based on the movement of your mouse. You can try Screen Studio for free, but it doesn't let you export the video file until you purchase a license.
3. SimpleScreenRecorder: An open source Linux program. One that’s easier to use than FFmpeg/avconv or VLC, due to its straightforward user interface.

### Loom alternatives for Android [...] Quick answer: The best Loom alternative depends on what you need after recording. If you want fast async video messages with more polished editing, AI cleanups, hosted links, team workspaces, analytics, and a simple way to switch from Loom, Tella is the best all-round option for creators and teams. If you only need local capture, open source software, or heavyweight editing, some of the other Loom competitors below may fit better.

## Why opt for a Loom alternative in the first place?

Initial commentary of choosing a Loom alternative included the sale to Atlassian. At the time, it was unclear how the product would evolve and be supported.

If you’re weighing up whether it’s actually worth moving, we’ve written a separate breakdown on why teams are switching from Loom.

results.title: Loom Alternatives in 2026: 20+ Tools Reviewed for Sales, Teams & Creators | Puppydog Blog
results.content:
| Tool | Free Tier Limits | Best For |
 --- 
| ScreenPal | Unlimited clips, 15-min cap, cloud hosting | Everyday async video |
| Fathom | Unlimited meeting recordings + transcripts | Meeting notes |
| OBS Studio | Unlimited, no watermark, no limits | Power users |
| Cap | 5-min shareable links (beta) | Open-source teams |
| Supademo | 5 interactive demos + 5 recordings | Interactive demos |
| Claap | 10 videos, 300 mins, 3-month storage | Async collaboration |
| Screenity | Unlimited recording, no account required | Chrome-based quick captures |

ScreenPal stands out as the most practical free replacement for Loom's everyday use cases. Cloud hosting, unlimited clips, and a 15-minute recording cap give you a working tool,  not a teaser.

## Best Loom Alternatives by Platform [...] ## Best Loom Alternatives by Platform

| Platform | Top Pick | Alternative | Key Reason |
 ---  --- |
| macOS | Screen Studio | Tella | Screen Studio uses macOS graphics for auto-zoom |
| Windows | ShareX | ScreenPal | Lightweight, hotkey-mapped, no performance lag |
| Linux | OBS Studio | Cap | Stable native Linux capture across main distros |
| Chrome extension | Screenity | Supademo | Free, no account needed to start recording |
| Android | tl;dv Mobile | Google Meet | Native mobile meeting capture |
| Offline recording | OBS Studio | Camtasia | Both encode locally without internet connection |
| Browser-only | VEED | Record by Wistia | No installation required, fully browser-based |

## Exact Pricing Comparison Table (Actual Dollar Amounts) [...] ### Step 4: Match your new tool to your actual workflow:

 Everyday async video: ScreenPal (free) or Tella
 Outbound sales demos and AI personalization: Puppydog.io
 Meeting recording and AI summaries: Fathom or tl;dv
 Professional training and e-learning: Camtasia or ScreenPal
 Interactive product tours: Supademo or Storylane
 Process documentation and SOPs: Glitter AI or Guidde
 Open-source and self-hosted: Cap

‍

## Conclusion

Loom earned its place in async video, and for casual internal communication, it still functions for plenty of teams. But the 2026 billing migration, rising reliability issues, and stagnant editing capabilities have made it genuinely difficult to defend on a budget review.

results.title: 8 Loom Alternatives We Tested (2026) | ngram.com
results.content:
### Best for

Pick Descript if you record demos, explainers, podcasts, webinars, or customer interviews and want transcript-first editing.

### Watch-outs

Descript can be more editor than messenger. For fast internal updates, Loom or Tella may feel lighter. For on-brand business-video generation from multiple inputs, ngram is more purpose-built.

## 4. Vidyard

Vidyard is the sales-led Loom alternative. Its current pricing page frames the product around revenue teams, with Free, Starter, Teams, Enterprise, and a Video Agent add-on for AI-personalized workflows. The page calls out CRM and marketing-automation integrations, branded sharing pages, CTAs, password protection, captions, team analytics, and secure playback depending on plan. [...] ## 7. Camtasia

Camtasia is the heavyweight desktop editor in this list. TechSmith's current support docs describe Essentials, Create, and Pro plan levels. Essentials focuses on screen recording and non-linear editing; Create adds text-based editing, AI-generated scripts, and audio cleanup; Pro adds audio-as-text editing, speech-to-text, localization, script generation, avatar generation, premium assets, and collaboration.

The fit is different from Loom. Camtasia is for people who expect to edit. Training teams, course creators, and documentation teams often need timeline control, captions, cursor effects, callouts, and repeatable tutorial production. Camtasia gives them that control, but it is not as frictionless as hitting record in Loom.

### Best for [...] Back to Alternatives
